response.justification: Bogdan has valuable geospatial domain expertise and distributed systems knowledge that align with the routing pipeline problem domain. However, his C++ experience is from 2014–2015, and recent work (2021–present) is primarily JavaScript/TypeScript rather than systems programming. His strong engineering discipline and systems-level thinking with DLang suggest adaptability, but the core requirement of modern C++ development needs careful assessment before investment.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise as GISCollective founder and Green Map developer, directly applicable to routing tile systems and map data handling
Proven distributed systems knowledge from Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec, microservices, low-latency asynchronous I/O, real-time metadata handling)
Strong engineering fundamentals with TDD discipline, testing expertise, Linux systems experience, and demonstrated ability to work beyond current expertise
concerns: C++ experience is over 10 years old (2014–2015 at National Instruments); recent 4+ years focused on JavaScript/TypeScript, risking skill atrophy in modern C++ standards
No demonstrated routing systems or navigation guidance experience; geospatial background is data collection platforms, not production routing pipelines or tile optimization
Systems programming background is in DLang, not C++ or Rust; would require substantial ramp-up on modern C++ standards, performance optimization, and navigation algorithms

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and real-time architecture experience
